conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have one child. Her name is Alex, she's 15 years old. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information based on the current rules.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I have the current eligibility rules. Let me ask you a few questions about Alex and your circumstances.

First, do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your living arrangements with Alex.

Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to understand Alex's current education or work status, as this is important for children aged 16 or over (though Alex is 15, it's good to confirm her situation).

Is Alex currently in full-time education or training, or has she started any paid work?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: She's still in school - doing her GCSEs at the moment. She doesn't have any paid work.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One final question: Is Alex currently in hospital or care for an extended period, or living with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually, I'm fostering Alex. But the local authority doesn't pay me anything for her accommodation or maintenance - I don't get anything from the council for fostering her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that important information. 

Just to confirm I understand correctly: you are fostering Alex, and the local council is not paying anything towards her accommodation or maintenance at all. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct. I'm fostering her, but the council isn't paying anything towards her accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with my decision:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16) and in full-time education
- She lives with you in the UK
- You are fostering her, and the eligibility rules specifically state that foster carers can receive Child Benefit as long as the local council is not paying anything towards the child's accommodation or maintenance
- Since the council isn't paying you anything for Alex's accommodation or maintenance, you qualify
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her

You can go ahead and apply for Child Benefit for Alex. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

You have confirmed that the local council is not paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
